question: Which wallets can I use on Stellar, and how do they differ?

keyFacts: Provides representative wallet/signing classes and correct operator/custody distinctions.
Explains comparison axes and makes multi-wallet integration conditional rather than mandatory.
avoid: Do NOT call a small example set exhaustive or 'main' without a defined usage/share metric.
Do NOT rank wallets by reconstructed SCF totals or marketing-scale claims.
Do NOT treat directory discovery as proof of current custody, platform, maintenance, or app-store availability.
golden.notes:
Directory discovery → scout_projects. This file OWNS the sponsor/model differentiation lane (Freighter=SDF, LOBSTR=Ultra Stellar, Hana multi-chain, xBull=Creit-Tech) — distinct from q-eco-stellar-wallets-list (flat enumeration probe) and q-eco-wallets-similar (find_similar routing). Added the directory-not-general-web guard as must_avoid (2026-06-29) to keep it in the directory lane. All grounded on Scout: Freighter (SDF official, browser+iOS/Android), LOBSTR (Ultra Stellar, SCF ~$232K), Hana (multi-chain, SCF ~$132K), xBull (Creit-Tech, SCF ~$56.2K). Devs use Stellar Wallets Kit to stay wallet-agnostic.
Also good if the answer: Notes scope differences: Freighter and xBull are Stellar-focused; LOBSTR and Hana are multi-chain (LOBSTR adds XRP Ledger; Hana is broadly multi-chain). | Notes developers should integrate the Stellar Wallets Kit to stay wallet-agnostic.
Golden cites: A source for each named wallet and its sponsor/model.
GT-20 CORRECTION 2026-07-10: grade representative examples, functional classes, comparison axes, and current primary verification—not an undefined 'main' roster or mandatory Wallets Kit recommendation. Wallet operator/platform/support facts are quarterly-freshness claims even though inherited tags cannot yet be overridden.

corroboration.claim: Freighter is an SDF/Stellar wallet; LOBSTR is an Ultra Stellar product; xBull is built under Creit-Tech; Hana supports Stellar in a multi-chain wallet

verified.by: Golden-truth sweep import — The prior correction removed reconstructed SCF totals, but GT-20 found that four examples were still presented as the 'main' wallets without a ranking metric, omitted functional classes, overstated Wallets Kit as a default, and carried false non-freshness/routing assumptions.
evidence: Solo scratchpad 575 GT-20 primary 3256 and blind 3257, 2026-07-10.
Sibling sweep covered q-eco-wallets-similar, q-eco-lobstr-wallet, and q-eco-stellar-wallets-list.
rootCause: Corpus-grounded directory rows were promoted into unqualified numeric facts in a case whose actual purpose is wallet sponsor/model differentiation.
improvements/stellar-light-scout/sls-011 — fixed basis disclosure does not make reconstructed project totals official.
